div.div: · special vase with internal thread mechanism

Effect: Three chosen cards are returned to the deck, placed in a vase, and then rise out of the vase one by one at your mental command. Method: the special vase has fine threads attached at specific positions inside. When each chosen card is controlled to its thread position, an almost invisible thread connects from the card to the performer's ring or a pin in their clothing. A tiny movement of the hand or body causes each card to rise dramatically from the vase. Three cards can rise in sequence with different subtle movements triggering each. The vase mechanism can be rigged at home with patience.

The Rising Cards is one of magic's oldest and most celebrated effects — the first documented mechanical rising card apparatus appeared in a French conjuring book in 1584. Modern versions use magnets, electronics, and remote controls, but the basic thread method still works beautifully.

Three chosen cards are returned to the deck which is placed in a vase. On command, each chosen card rises mysteriously from the vase. The mechanism uses threads and small pulleys inside the vase — the performer triggers each rise by a concealed hair or thread attachment to their body.

svg.text: Source: The Magician's Own Book (1862) · Heritage Parlor
